graph_metadata.matter: Tata Capital Limited v. Deepak Agarwal
graph_metadata.case_reference: Case No. TCL//18923//2026; Contract No. TCFBL0370000012362883
graph_metadata.ontology: Loan Arbitration Graph Grounding — 13 base node types + 4 extensions (all DARK in this instance)
graph_metadata.purpose: Grounding data for Claimant's Rejoinder to the Respondent's reply
graph_metadata.role: Held-out fourth instance (skill-creator validation). Second occurrence of the informal x inter_partes -> Merits cell; surfaces the co-borrower-caption mistake and the different-lis / accelerated-quantum refinements.
graph_metadata.procedural_posture: MERITS. Respondent engaged via an advocate's letter (Emkay Solutions / Adv. Vaibhav Dwivedi, 25-Mar-2026) captioned a REPLY to a Section 25 Payment & Settlement Systems Act, 2007 demand notice — NOT to the SOC or the arbitration commencement notice. Pleads medical hardship, sole-provider status, seeks settlement, asserts a right to deactivate the ECS/NACH mandate. Tribunal proceeded inter-partes; Claimant filed a merits rejoinder (16-Jun-2026) engaging the reply. Branch = Merits (driven by proceeding_mode). Same matrix cell as Tata-Gupta.
positive_controls: name_change_proof IN RECORD (NCLT order 24-Nov-2023) — the name_change_proof_not_in_record flag correctly does NOT fire here, contrast Gupta
all four extension triggers correctly dark (negative control holds a second time)

nodes.label: Loan

props.product: Business Loan Unsec — Prospect PLPA SE OTF STP
props.defaultAdditionalInterest: 36% p.a. (3% per month, agreement Loan-details section)
props.totalOutstanding_operative_basis: Stable across SOA (13-Feb), commencement notice and SOC; rejoinder pleads no different figure. As-on 13-Feb-2026.

quantumComposition.note: Only 25,422 is overdue; the 90,499 balance principal is the unmatured body brought forward by RECALL/ACCELERATION. The claim is the whole accelerated outstanding, not the overdue installments — defends against a 'only ~5 EMIs overdue' objection.

flags: quantum_unrebutted_lis_not_joined: the respondent's reply answered a S.25 PSS Act demand, not the SOC; the Rs. 1,16,748 claim and its acceleration are NOT traversed and stand unrebutted on the record.
recall_vs_claim_progression: LRN recall 1,13,021 (as-on 16-Jan) vs claim 1,16,748 (as-on 13-Feb) — one further cycle of installment + penal + interest; explainable stage progression.

nodes.label: SecuredCreditor

props.nameChange: Formerly Tata Capital Financial Services Ltd
props.nameChangeMechanism: NCLT-sanctioned Scheme of Arrangement merging TCFSL + Tata Cleantech Capital Ltd into TCL
props.nameChangeProof: IN RECORD — NCLT order dated 24-Nov-2023, effective 01-Jan-2024
props.entityType: Non-Banking Financial Company, Companies Act 1956, RBI-registered
props.registeredOffice: 11th Floor, Tower A, Peninsula Business Park, Lower Parel, Mumbai 400013

flags: name_change_proof_in_record (POSITIVE CONTROL): unlike Gupta, the merger is evidenced (NCLT order pleaded and listed as document #9). The name_change_proof_not_in_record flag correctly does NOT fire.

props.address: F-9 Friends Garden, Sikandra, Agra, Uttar Pradesh 282007

nodes.label: CoBorrower

props.note: SOA 'Co-Applicant Name: NA'; agreement single borrower. The rejoinder cause-title reads 'Deepak Agarwal & othr.' — a PHANTOM second respondent contradicted by the SOA. Caption boilerplate, NOT a co-borrower. Per the corroboration rule, CoBorrower stays suppressed.

flags: phantom_co_respondent_in_caption: '& othr' in the rejoinder caption with no corroborating co-applicant in the SOA/agreement. Do not activate CoBorrower on the caption alone.

props.note: UNSECURED business loan. SOA: 'There is no Collateral Information.' No hypothecation. (Consistent: the LRN does not demand asset surrender — contrast a vehicle loan.)

nodes.label: Communication

props.content: Recites the NCLT merger (24-Nov-2023, effective 01-Jan-2024); recalls loan; Rs. 1,13,021 due as-on 16-Jan-2026; 7-day demand; invokes arbitration

flags: criminal_threat_boilerplate_on_civil_default: LRN paras 5 & 8 allege 'fraud', 'cheating and criminal breach of trust' and threaten a criminal complaint under BNS 2023 ss. 316/322/61/3(5) on a simple unsecured business-loan default — over-reach; the recall-notice boilerplate does not fit a civil default. Drafter should not rely on the criminal characterisation in the arbitration.

props.content: Lists 11 institutions; 7-day nomination / deemed acceptance; outstanding stated Rs. 1,16,748

props.type: Section 25 PSS Act demand notice (referenced, not produced)
props.note: The respondent's reply is captioned a response to a 'Notice of Demand under Section 25 of the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007'. That demand notice is NOT in the bundle. Its existence is inferred only from the reply's caption.

nodes.label: Communication

props.issuedBy: Adv. Vaibhav Dwivedi, Emkay Solutions, Noida
props.addressedTo: Tata Capital (Mumbai Fort address / customercare email)
props.answersWhichLis: A Section 25 PSS Act demand notice — NOT the SOC or the arbitration commencement notice
contentions: Admits availing the business loan; bona fide intention to repay
Pleads medical condition and severe financial difficulty; sole provider for family
Seeks a mutually beneficial settlement of the outstanding balance
Asserts a right to deactivate the ECS/NACH mandate due to financial crunch, citing RBI guidelines
Asks the claimant not to visit residence/office

flags: reply_addresses_different_lis: this reply answers a S.25 PSS Act demand, so neither it nor the rejoinder is joined to the SOC's claim. The SOC quantum/acceleration is therefore unrebutted; the rejoinder is in substance litigating the ECS-mandate / hardship points raised against a different notice.

nodes.label: ArbitrationProceeding

props.arbitrationInstitution: Centre for Alternative Dispute Resolution Excellence (CADRE)
props.appointmentMechanism: Claimant's unilateral 11-institution list; deemed acceptance on borrower silence within 7 days; institution appoints
props.seat: Chennai (Agreement Clause 10: 'seat of arbitration for all purposes shall be at Chennai'; SOC para 12)
props.costAllocation: Borne by the Borrower (Agreement Clause 10: 'The cost of arbitration shall be borne by the Borrower')

flags: arbitration_clause_number_mismatch: SOC (paras 10 & 12) pleads the arbitration clause as 'clause 9'; the agreement's arbitration clause is Clause 10 (clause 9 is 'Other Conditions'). Correct the reference.
unilateral_appointment_available_unchallenged: the unilateral-list + deemed-acceptance mechanism is a potential independence challenge; the respondent did not raise it.

props.note: No actual S.138 NI Act proceeding. The rejoinder invokes S.138 NI Act and S.25 PSS Act only rhetorically, as consequences of ECS deactivation. Ontology holds; node stays suppressed.

issues_to_relevance_map.issue: Quantum / acceleration — claim Rs. 1,16,748 (SOC paras 9-11). NOTE: not traversed by the reply.
issues_to_relevance_map.claimant_thrust: On recall, the whole accelerated outstanding is due, not just the overdue EMIs; composition shown
Hardship does not legally extinguish a commercial debt; early good conduct does not absolve

issues_to_relevance_map.issue: ECS/NACH mandate deactivation asserted as a borrower right under RBI guidelines (Reply)
issues_to_relevance_map.claimant_thrust: An ECS/NACH mandate is a binding authorisation to service an active debt; unilateral deactivation to evade EMIs is a deliberate default (S.25 PSS Act; S.138 NI Act consequences)

issues_to_relevance_map.issue: Foundation / locus — claimant identity post name-change
issues_to_relevance_map.claimant_thrust: TCL is the merged transferee of TCFSL per the NCLT order; locus established

coverage_check.name_change_locus: Backed — NCLT order dated 24-Nov-2023 (positive control; flag does not fire)
coverage_check.lis_warning: reply_addresses_different_lis — do not treat the SOC merits as joined; the quantum claim is unrebutted on the record.
coverage_check.extension_nodes_flagged: n/a — all four extensions dark (negative control)
